name: Coefficients of modular function j as power series in q = e^(2 Pi i t). Another name is the elliptic modular invariant J(tau).
formula: It appears that -n * a(n) = A035230(n). - _Gerald McGarvey_, Dec 21 2006
Expansion of 128 * (theta_2(q)^8 + theta_3(q)^8 + theta_4(q)^8) * (theta_2(q)^-8 + theta_3(q)^-8 + theta_4(q)^-8) in powers of q^2. - _Michael Somos_, Oct 02 2007
G.f.: 256*(1 - lambda + lambda^2)^3/(lambda^2 * (1 - lambda)^2) where lambda is the elliptic modular function (A115977). - _Seiichi Manyama_, Jul 30 2017
Conjecture: Sum_{k>=-1} a(k) / exp(k*Pi) = 66^3 = 287496. - _Simon Plouffe_, Apr 01 2026

The above conjecture is correct. Note that the LHS is the expansion of j(I/2), and the section "Definition" of the Wikipedia page on the j-invariant contains the example j(2*I) = 66^3. Since the j-invariant is an automophism form for SL(2,Z), we have j(I/2) = j(-1/(2*I)) = j(2*I) = 66^3. - _Jianing Song_, Apr 08 2026
program: (PARI) {a(n) = my(A); if( n<-1, 0, A = x^(2*n + 2) * O(x); A = x * (eta(x + A) * eta(x^4 + A)^2 / eta(x^2 + A)^3)^8; polcoeff( subst( 256 * (1 - x + x^2)^3 / (x - x^2)^2, x, 16*A), 2*n))}; /* _Michael Somos_, Apr 30 2004 */
(PARI) {a(n) = my(A); if( n<-1, 0, A = x^(5*n + 5) * O(x); A = (eta(x + A) / eta(x^5 + A))^6 / x; polcoeff( subst( (x^2 + 10*x + 5)^3 / x, x, A), 5*n))}; /* _Michael Somos_, Apr 30 2004 */
(PARI) {a(n) = my(A); if( n<-1, 0, A = x^2 * O(x^n); A = x * (eta(x^2 + A) / eta(x + A))^24; polcoeff( (1 + 256*A)^3 / A, n))}; /* _Michael Somos_, Jul 13 2004 */
(PARI) {a(n) = if( n<-1, 0, polcoeff( ellj(x + x^3 * O(x^n)), n))}; /* _Michael Somos_, Dec 25 2016 */
xref: Cf. also A161361, A161362, A161395, A178451, A339429 (indices with prime values).
